english: crete nouns and the masculine gender, frq., in contradistinction to abstract nouns with -pa (pa) or -ba (ba), and to feminines with -mo (mo); connected with a numeral, it supplies the definite article: lṅá-po (lnga po) the five (just mentioned); gñis-po (gnyis po) the two, both, = gñis-ka (gnyis ka). - 2. num. figure: 133.

(Ssk. पोत ship, ल to receive, hence: harbour, port; Tib. gru-dźin (gru 'dzin)) 1. ancient n. of Tatta, a town not far from the mouth of the Indus. - 2. n. of a three-peaked hill near Lhasa, with the palace of the old kings of Tibet, now the seat of the Dalai Lama. (The spelling 'Buddha-la' arises from an erroneous etymological hypothesis, and the fact of its being found even in Huc's writings may be attributed merely to a thoughtless adherence to what had become a custom; v. Köpp. II, 340.)
(acc. to one Lex. a corruption of pu-sta-ka (pu sta ka), for which also the form pu-sti (pu sti) seems to speak) = glegs-bam (glegs bam), book (of loose leaves).
Sik. large wasp.
C. bullock.
v. mt'o-po-tog (mtho po tog).

the well-known Turkish mess of pilaw, Hind. pulao, rice boiled with fowl; in Ld. however sweet rice, prepared with butter, sugar, and 'pating'; fig. bsám-bloi pó-la byéd-pa (bsam blo'i po la byed pa) to concoct and deal in plans and plots.
n. of the mountains bordering on China Ld.-Glr. Schl. 21, a (where in the translation the word has not been recognized as being a proper name).
censer, perfuming-pan.
v. pugs-ta (phugs ta).
v. ṗod (phod), ṗon (phon), ṗón-to (phon to).
C. castrated ram.
Ts. = tsá-bai nad (tsha ba'i nad).
small turkoises, 1 or 2''' in size, strung together for finger-rings, v. tsom (tshom).
Cs. 1. lot; pra ₒdébs-pa (pra 'debs pa) to cast lot. - 2. sign, token, prognostic; Sch.: pra ₒbebs-pa (pra 'bebs pa) 'ein Zeichen geben, ein Bild darstellen'.
Lex. w.e. Sch. jest, joke, fun, nonsensical talk; byéd-pa (byed pa) to make sport, to play the buffoon; slóṅ-ba (slong ba) to cause merriment; pra-čál-pa (pra cal pa), or -mǩan (-mkhan) wag, buffoon.
Sch.: hill-mouse (marmot?), hare (?); cf. brá-ba (bra ba).

an alpine herb, said to be very wholesome to sheep (so for instance in Purig); acc. to recent investigations, of little value. Acc. to Cs. = á-kroṅ (a khrong), but this is denied by the people of Lahoul.
Ssk., n. of several kinds of Indian aromatic plants Med.
Lex., the crest of a cock Cs.; próg-žu (prog zhu), bróg-žu (brog zhu), spróg-žu (sprog zhu) = čod-pán (cod pan).

(शूर, वीर), also spá-ba (spa ba) 1. bravery, strength, courage; brave, strong, courageous; dpa bgóṅ-ba (dpa' bgong ba) Lex., ǩóṅ-ba (khong ba) Thgy.; góṅ-ba (gong ba), bkóṅ-ba (bkong ba) Lex., to despond; to dishearten (?); śin-tu dpá-žiṅ (shin tu dpa' zhing) he becoming very brave Dzl.; dpá-la stobs kyaṅ gyad daṅ bñám-ste (dpa' la stobs kyang gyad dang bnyam ste) being brave, and in strength equal to an athlete Dzl. - 2. beauty; beautiful. - 3. W. taste, agreeable taste, flavour. Comp. dpá-čan (dpa' can) 1. brave. 2. beautiful. 3. W. savoury. - dpa-méd-kăn (dpa' med mkhan) W. tasteless, v. also ldád-pa (ldad pa). - dpa-čén (dpa' chen) very brave; a great hero. - dpa-dár (dpa' dar) = mgul-dár (mgul dar), a piece of silk, tied round the neck, as an honourable distinction for some brave deed. - dpa-ldán (dpa' ldan) = dpá-čan (dpa' can) 1 and 2. - dpá-bo (dpa' bo), शूर, 1. strong man, hero. 2. demigod. - dpa-bo-dkár (dpa' bo dkar) a medicinal herb Med. - dpá-mo (dpa' mo) 1. heroine (more frq. than the masc. dpá-bo (dpa' bo)). 2. = mǩa-gro-ma (mkha' 'gro ma), Dā-kini Mil., Thgr., Glr. - dpa-tsúl (dpa' tshul) Mil. = dpá-ba (dpa' ba) 1. sbst., ni f.
